ARMOR Error Response Header Consistency Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document verifies and documents the consistency of HTTP response headers across all rejection/error scenarios in ARMOR.\n\n## Analysis Date\n\n2026-07-14\n\n## Error Response Implementation\n\n### Response Writers\n\nARMOR has two `writeError` functions that handle error responses:\n\n1. **`internal/server/server.go:writeError`** - Handles authentication/authorization errors\n2. **`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:writeError`** - Handles S3 operation errors\n\nBoth implementations are **identical**:\n\n```go\nfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    var codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n    xml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n    xml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n        codeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n```\n\n### Response Headers Set\n\n**All error responses set exactly two headers:**\n\n1. **`Content-Type: application/xml`** - Consistently set for all error types\n2. **HTTP Status Code** - Varies by error category (see below)\n\n### Response Format\n\nAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format:\n\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>ERROR_CODE</Code>\n  <Message>Error message description</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Error Categories and HTTP Status Codes\n\n### 1. Authentication/Authorization Errors (403 Forbidden)\n\n| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | Authorization header is missing | \"Missing Authentication Token\" |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | Invalid access key provided | \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\" |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | Calculated signature doesn't match | \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\" |\n| `RequestExpired` | Request timestamp outside 15-minute window | \"Request has expired\" |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm | \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\" |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | Authorization header missing fields | \"Authorization header is missing required fields\" |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | X-Amz-Date header missing | \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\" |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | X-Amz-Date format invalid | \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\" |\n| `AccessDenied` | ACL-based access control rejection | \"Access Denied\" |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 403`\n\n### 2. Client Input Errors (400 Bad Request / 405 Method Not Allowed)\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------|\n| `InvalidRequest` | 400 | Unsupported POST operation, missing/invalid parameters | Varies |\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | HTTP method not supported | \"Method {method} not allowed\" |\n| `InvalidRange` | 400 | Invalid Range header format | \"Invalid range: {details}\" |\n| `MalformedXML` | 400 | Failed to parse request XML | \"Failed to parse XML: {details}\" |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 400` or `HTTP/1.1 405`\n\n### 3. Resource Not Found Errors (404 Not Found)\n\n| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `NoSuchKey` | Object does not exist | \"Object not found\" |\n| `NoSuchBucket` | Bucket does not exist | \"Bucket not found\" |\n| `NoSuchUpload` | Multipart upload ID not found | \"Multipart upload not found\" |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 404`\n\n### 4. Conditional Request Errors (412 Precondition Failed)\n\n| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `PreconditionFailed` | If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed | \"Precondition failed\" |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 412`\n\n### 5. Internal Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error)\n\n| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `InternalError` | Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors | Varies: \"Failed to {operation}: {error}\" |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 500`\n\n### 6. Service Unavailable (503 Service Unavailable)\n\n| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `ServiceUnavailable` | Health check failures | Varies |\n\n**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 503`\n\n## Consistency Verification\n\n### ✅ Consistent Headers\n\nAll error responses across **all rejection types** consistently set:\n- **`Content-Type: application/xml`** - Always set, no exceptions\n- **HTTP status code** - Appropriate for error category\n\n### ✅ Consistent Response Format\n\nAll error responses follow the same XML structure:\n- XML declaration: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n- Root element: `<Error>`\n- Code element: `<Code>`\n- Message element: `<Message>`\n\n### ✅ XML Escaping\n\nBoth `writeError` implementations properly escape XML special characters in code and message fields, preventing injection attacks.\n\n## Potential Minor Inconsistencies\n\n### 1. Message Format Variation\n\n**Status:** ⚠️ Minor inconsistency in message verbosity\n\nSome error messages include detailed error context (e.g., `\"Failed to encrypt: {error}\"`), while others are static strings (e.g., `\"Access Denied\"`). This is **intentional and appropriate** - internal errors need diagnostics, while auth errors should be generic for security.\n\n**Recommendation:** No change needed - this variation is security best practice.\n\n### 2. Error Code Casing\n\n**Status:** ✅ Consistent\n\nAll error codes use PascalCase (e.g., `SignatureDoesNotMatch`, `InternalError`), matching AWS S3 conventions.\n\n### 3. Duplicate writeError Implementations\n\n**Status:** ⚠️ Code duplication\n\nBoth `server.go` and `handlers.go` contain identical `writeError` functions. This creates maintenance risk if one is updated without the other.\n\n**Recommendation:** Consider extracting to a shared utility function.\n\n## Header Consistency Summary Table\n\n| Error Category | HTTP Status | Content-Type | Other Headers |\n|----------------|-------------|--------------|---------------|\n| Authentication failures | 403 | application/xml | None |\n| Authorization failures | 403 | application/xml | None |\n| Input validation errors | 400 | application/xml | None |\n| Method not allowed | 405 | application/xml | None |\n| Resource not found | 404 | application/xml | None |\n| Precondition failed | 412 | application/xml | None |\n| Internal errors | 500 | application/xml | None |\n| Service unavailable | 503 | application/xml | None |\n\n## Verification Results\n\n### Acceptance Criteria Status\n\n| Criterion | Status | Notes |\n|-----------|--------|-------|\n| Headers documented for each rejection scenario | ✅ Complete | All error types documented with headers |\n| Inconsistent headers identified and documented | ✅ Complete | No functional inconsistencies found; only minor code duplication noted |\n| Header consistency verified | ✅ Verified | All responses use `Content-Type: application/xml` consistently |\n\n### Performance\n\nError responses are consistently fast:\n- No additional headers added beyond `Content-Type`\n- Response time dominated by authentication verification, not header setting\n- Single `WriteHeader` call per response (no multiple writes)\n\n## Conclusion\n\nARMOR error response headers are **fully consistent** across all rejection types:\n\n1. ✅ All error responses set exactly the same headers (`Content-Type: application/xml` plus HTTP status)\n2. ✅ Header values are consistent and appropriate for each error category\n3. ✅ Response format is standardized S3 XML error structure\n4. ✅ No functional inconsistencies found\n\nThe only minor issue is code duplication between two identical `writeError` functions, which is a maintainability concern but not a functional inconsistency.\n\n**Overall Assessment: PASS** - Error response header consistency is properly implemented across all rejection scenarios.\n","numLines":202,"startLine":1,"totalLines":202}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_27cc4c818b614253ba58e8ad","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR Error Response Documentation\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tARMOR provides S3-compatible error responses for all authentication, authorization, and request processing failures. This document describes the error response format, all error codes, rejection scenarios, performance characteristics, and examples.\n6\t\n7\t## Quick Reference: All Error Codes\n8\t\n9\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Category | Description |\n10\t|------------|-------------|----------|-------------|\n11\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header or X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n12\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | Authentication | The provided access key does not exist in credentials store |\n13\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | Authentication | Calculated signature does not match the provided signature |\n14\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | Authentication | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported |\n15\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Authentication | Credential format is invalid (insufficient parts) |\n16\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header is missing required fields |\n17\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Authentication | Request timestamp is outside allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n18\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n19\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header format is invalid (not ISO 8601) |\n20\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authorization | ACL restrictions prevent access to requested bucket/key |\n21\t| `InvalidRequest` | 400 | Request | Invalid request parameters or unsupported operation |\n22\t| `NoSuchKey` | 404 | Request | Requested object does not exist in the bucket |\n23\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Request | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n24\t| `PreconditionFailed` | 412 | Request | Conditional request precondition failed |\n25\t| `InternalError` | 500 | Server | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n26\t\n27\t## Error Response Format\n28\t\n29\t## Error Response Format\n30\t\n31\tAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n32\t\n33\t### HTTP Headers\n34\t\n35\t```http\n36\tContent-Type: application/xml\n37\tStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n38\t```\n39\t\n40\t### Response Body\n41\t\n42\t```xml\n43\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n44\t<Error>\n45\t  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n46\t  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n47\t</Error>\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\t## Authentication Error Codes\n51\t\n52\t### Invalid Credentials\n53\t\n54\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n55\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n56\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n57\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n58\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n59\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n60\t\n61\t### Malformed Authorization Headers\n62\t\n63\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n64\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n65\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n66\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n67\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n68\t\n69\t### Timing Issues\n70\t\n71\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n72\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n73\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n74\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n75\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header. | X-Amz-Date header is not in ISO 8601 format (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) |\n76\t\n77\t## Authorization and Access Control Errors\n78\t\n79\t### ACL-Based Access Denial\n80\t\n81\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n82\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n83\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Credential exists but ACL restrictions prevent access to the requested bucket/key |\n84\t\n85\t**ACL Access Control Scenarios:**\n86\t\n87\tARMOR supports bucket and prefix-based access control lists (ACLs). When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n88\t\n89\t1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n90\t2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n91\t3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n92\t\n93\t**Example:**\n94\t\n95\t```yaml\n96\t# Credential configuration\n97\tcredentials:\n98\t  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n99\t    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n100\t    secret_key: SECRET123...\n101\t    acls:\n102\t      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n103\t        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\tWith this configuration:\n107\t- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n108\t- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n109\t- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n110\t\n111\t## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n112\t\n113\tARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n114\t\n115\t| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n116\t|----------|------------|-------------|\n117\t| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n118\t| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n119\t| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n120\t| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n121\t\n122\t## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n123\t\n124\tARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. These scenarios can result in authentication errors:\n125\t\n126\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n127\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n128\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing Authentication Token | X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n129\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format | X-Amz-Credential has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n130\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key from credential parameter not found |\n131\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authorization header is missing required fields | X-Amz-SignedHeaders or X-Amz-Signature query parameter is missing |\n132\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired | Presigned URL has exceeded its expiration time (X-Amz-Expires) |\n133\t\n134\t**Presigned URL Format:**\n135\t```\n136\thttps://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/key?\n137\t  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n138\t  X-Amz-Credential=ACCESSKEY/DATE/REGION/s3/aws4_request&\n139\t  X-Amz-Date=YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ&\n140\t  X-Amz-Expires=SECONDS&\n141\t  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n142\t  X-Amz-Signature=CALCULATED_SIGNATURE\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\t## Request Method Errors\n146\t\n147\tARMOR validates that the HTTP method is appropriate for the requested operation:\n148\t\n149\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n150\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n151\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Method {METHOD} not allowed | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n152\t\n153\t**Examples:**\n154\t- Unsupported POST operation on endpoints that only support GET\n155\t- Using DELETE on read-only operations\n156\t\n157\t## Internal Server Errors\n158\t\n159\tThese errors indicate server-side problems during request processing:\n160\t\n161\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n162\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n163\t| `InternalError` | 500 | Failed to {operation}: {error} | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n164\t\n165\t**Common InternalError Scenarios:**\n166\t- Encryption key derivation failures\n167\t- DEK generation failures\n168\t- IV generation failures\n169\t- Header encoding failures\n170\t- Upload failures\n171\t- Temporary file creation failures\n172\t\n173\t## Performance Guarantees\n174\t\n175\tAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n176\t\n177\t| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n178\t|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n179\t| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n180\t| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n181\t| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n182\t\n183\t### Performance Test Coverage\n184\t\n185\tThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n186\t\n187\t1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n188\t2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n189\t\n190\t## Error Message Quality\n191\t\n192\tAll error responses include:\n193\t\n194\t1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n195\t2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n196\t3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n197\t\n198\t### Message Validation\n199\t\n200\tTest suite verifies that:\n201\t- Error messages are never empty\n202\t- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n203\t- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n204\t- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n205\t\n206\t## Response Consistency\n207\t\n208\tAll error responses maintain consistency:\n209\t\n210\t### Headers\n211\t- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n212\t- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n213\t- Response body is never empty\n214\t\n215\t### Structure\n216\t- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n217\t- Root element: `<Error>`\n218\t- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n219\t- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n220\t\n221\t## Test Coverage Summary\n222\t\n223\tThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n224\t\n225\t### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n226\t- 12 test scenarios covering:\n227\t  - Invalid AWS credentials\n228\t  - Malformed signatures  \n229\t  - Missing authentication headers\n230\t  - Malformed authorization headers\n231\t  - Insufficient credential parts\n232\t  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n233\t  - Expired requests\n234\t  - Performance validation\n235\t\n236\t### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n237\t- 20+ test scenarios /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n275\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n276\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\n277\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n278\t```\n279\t\n280\t**Response:**\n281\t```http\n282\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n283\tContent-Type: application/xml\n284\t\n285\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n286\t<Error>\n287\t  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n288\t  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records.</Message>\n289\t</Error>\n290\t```\n291\t\n292\t### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n293\t\n294\t**Request:**\n295\t```http\n296\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n297\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t**Response:**\n301\t```http\n302\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n303\tContent-Type: application/xml\n304\t\n305\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n306\t<Error>\n307\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n308\t  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n309\t</Error>\n310\t```\n311\t\n312\t### Example 3: Expired Request\n313\t\n314\t**Request:**\n315\t```http\n316\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n317\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n318\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\n319\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n320\t```\n321\t\n322\t**Response:**\n323\t```http\n324\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n325\tContent-Type: application/xml\n326\t\n327\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n328\t<Error>\n329\t  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n330\t  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n331\t</Error>\n332\t```\n333\t\n334\t### Example 4: ACL Access Denied\n335\t\n336\t**Request:**\n337\t```http\n338\tGET /protected-bucket/admin/config.yaml HTTP/1.1\n339\tHost: protected-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n340\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=RESTRICTEDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\n341\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n342\t```\n343\t\n344\t**Configuration:**\n345\t```yaml\n346\tcredentials:\n347\t  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n348\t    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n349\t    secret_key: SECRET123...\n350\t    acls:\n351\t      - bucket: \"protected-bucket\"\n352\t        prefix: \"public/\"\n353\t```\n354\t\n355\t**Response:**\n356\t```http\n357\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n358\tContent-Type: application/xml\n359\t\n360\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n361\t<Error>\n362\t  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n363\t  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n364\t</Error>\n365\t```\n366\t\n367\t### Example 5: Expired Presigned URL\n368\t\n369\t**Request:**\n370\t```http\n371\tGET /data-bucket/file.csv?\n372\t  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n373\t  X-Amz-Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250701/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request&\n374\t  X-Amz-Date=20250701T120000Z&\n375\t  X-Amz-Expires=3600&\n376\t  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n377\t  X-Amz-Signature=abc123...\n378\tHTTP/1.1\n379\tHost: data-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n380\t```\n381\t\n382\t**Response:**\n383\t```http\n384\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n385\tContent-Type: application/xml\n386\t\n387\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n388\t<Error>\n389\t  <Code>RequestExpired</Code>\n390\t  <Message>Request has expired</Message>\n391\t</Error>\n392\t```\n393\t\n394\t### Example 6: Invalid Date Format\n395\t\n396\t**Request:**\n397\t```http\n398\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n399\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n400\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\n401\tX-Amz-Date: July-14-2025\n402\t```\n403\t\n404\t**Response:**\n405\t```http\n406\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n407\tContent-Type: application/xml\n408\t\n409\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n410\t<Error>\n411\t  <Code>InvalidDateFormat</Code>\n412\t  <Message>Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header</Message>\n413\t</Error>\n414\t```\n415\t\n416\t## Implementation\n417\t\n418\tError responses are generated by two implementations:\n419\t\n420\t1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n421\t   ```go\n422\t   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n423\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n424\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n425\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n426\t   }\n427\t   ```\n428\t\n429\t2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n430\t   ```go\n431\t   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n432\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n433\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n434\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n435\t   }\n436\t   ```\n437\t\n438\tBoth implementations ensure:\n439\t- Consistent Content-Type header\n440\t- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n441\t- S3-compatible format\n442\t\n443\t## Testing\n444\t\n445\tTo run the error response test suite:\n446\t\n447\t```bash\n448\t# Run all rejection tests\n449\tgo test -v -run \"TestInvalidCredentialRejection|TestMalformedSignatureRejection\" ./internal/server/\n450\t\n451\t# Run headers consistency test\n452\tgo test -v -run TestErrorResponseHeadersConsistency ./internal/server/\n453\t\n454\t# Run integration tests (requires INTEGRATION_TEST=1)\n455\tINTEGRATION_TEST=1 go test -v -run TestInvalidCredentialsIntegration ./internal/server/\n456\t```\n457\t\n458\t## Maintenance\n459\t\n460\tWhen adding new error scenarios:\n461\t\n462\t1. **Add test coverage** - Create tests in appropriate test file\n463\t2. **Verify ARMOR Error Response Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nARMOR provides S3-compatible error responses for all authentication, authorization, and request processing failures. This document describes the error response format, all error codes, rejection scenarios, performance characteristics, and examples.\n\n## Quick Reference: All Error Codes\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Category | Description |\n|------------|-------------|----------|-------------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header or X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | Authentication | The provided access key does not exist in credentials store |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | Authentication | Calculated signature does not match the provided signature |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | Authentication | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Authentication | Credential format is invalid (insufficient parts) |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header is missing required fields |\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Authentication | Request timestamp is outside allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header format is invalid (not ISO 8601) |\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authorization | ACL restrictions prevent access to requested bucket/key |\n| `InvalidRequest` | 400 | Request | Invalid request parameters or unsupported operation |\n| `NoSuchKey` | 404 | Request | Requested object does not exist in the bucket |\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Request | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n| `PreconditionFailed` | 412 | Request | Conditional request precondition failed |\n| `InternalError` | 500 | Server | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n\n## Error Response Format\n\n## Error Response Format\n\nAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n\n### HTTP Headers\n\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n```\n\n### Response Body\n\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Authentication Error Codes\n\n### Invalid Credentials\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n\n### Malformed Authorization Headers\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n\n### Timing Issues\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header. | X-Amz-Date header is not in ISO 8601 format (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) |\n\n## Authorization and Access Control Errors\n\n### ACL-Based Access Denial\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Credential exists but ACL restrictions prevent access to the requested bucket/key |\n\n**ACL Access Control Scenarios:**\n\nARMOR supports bucket and prefix-based access control lists (ACLs). When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n\n1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n\n**Example:**\n\n```yaml\n# Credential configuration\ncredentials:\n  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n    secret_key: SECRET123...\n    acls:\n      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n```\n\nWith this configuration:\n- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n\n## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n\nARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n\n| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n|----------|------------|-------------|\n| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n\n## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n\nARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. These scenarios can result in authentication errors:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing Authentication Token | X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format | X-Amz-Credential has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key from credential parameter not found |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authorization header is missing required fields | X-Amz-SignedHeaders or X-Amz-Signature query parameter is missing |\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired | Presigned URL has exceeded its expiration time (X-Amz-Expires) |\n\n**Presigned URL Format:**\n```\nhttps://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/key?\n  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n  X-Amz-Credential=ACCESSKEY/DATE/REGION/s3/aws4_request&\n  X-Amz-Date=YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ&\n  X-Amz-Expires=SECONDS&\n  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n  X-Amz-Signature=CALCULATED_SIGNATURE\n```\n\n## Request Method Errors\n\nARMOR validates that the HTTP method is appropriate for the requested operation:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Method {METHOD} not allowed | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n\n**Examples:**\n- Unsupported POST operation on endpoints that only support GET\n- Using DELETE on read-only operations\n\n## Internal Server Errors\n\nThese errors indicate server-side problems during request processing:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `InternalError` | 500 | Failed to {operation}: {error} | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n\n**Common InternalError Scenarios:**\n- Encryption key derivation failures\n- DEK generation failures\n- IV generation failures\n- Header encoding failures\n- Upload failures\n- Temporary file creation failures\n\n## Performance Guarantees\n\nAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n\n| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n\n### Performance Test Coverage\n\nThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n\n1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n\n## Error Message Quality\n\nAll error responses include:\n\n1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n\n### Message Validation\n\nTest suite verifies that:\n- Error messages are never empty\n- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n\n## Response Consistency\n\nAll error responses maintain consistency:\n\n### Headers\n- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n- Response body is never empty\n\n### Structure\n- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n- Root element: `<Error>`\n- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n\n## Test Coverage Summary\n\nThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n\n### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n- 12 test scenarios covering:\n  - Invalid AWS credentials\n  - Malformed signatures  \n  - Missing authentication headers\n  - Malformed authorization headers\n  - Insufficient credential parts\n  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n  - Expired requests\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n- 20+ test scenarios covering:\n  - Garbage signature strings (non-hex, too short, empty, random chars)\n  - Invalid signature formats (missing algorithm, wrong algorithm, missing components)\n  - Partial signatures (missing components)\n  - Error message quality\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Unit Tests (`error_response_verification_test.go`)\n- Comprehensive verification of all acceptance criteria:\n  - Meaningful error messages for all rejection scenarios\n  - Error messages specify the rejection reason\n  - Response time under 100ms for all rejections\n  - Consistent response headers across rejection types\n  - Performance statistics and thresholds\n  - Documentation generation for error response format\n\n### Integration Tests (`invalid_credential_integration_test.go`)\n- Real server tests with actual HTTP client\n- Performance validation under realistic conditions\n- End-to-end verification of error responses\n\n### Headers Consistency (`error_response_test.go`)\n- Verifies consistent headers across all rejection types\n- Validates Content-Type header\n- Ensures proper XML structure\n\n### Authorization Tests (`auth_headers_doc_test.go`)\n- Documents authentication rejection response headers\n- Generates comprehensive header documentation\n- Verifies error codes and messages for all auth scenarios\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: Invalid Access Key\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 3: Expired Request\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 4: ACL Access Denied\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /protected-bucket/admin/config.yaml HTTP/1.1\nHost: protected-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=RESTRICTEDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Configuration:**\n```yaml\ncredentials:\n  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n    secret_key: SECRET123...\n    acls:\n      - bucket: \"protected-bucket\"\n        prefix: \"public/\"\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 5: Expired Presigned URL\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /data-bucket/file.csv?\n  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n  X-Amz-Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250701/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request&\n  X-Amz-Date=20250701T120000Z&\n  X-Amz-Expires=3600&\n  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n  X-Amz-Signature=abc123...\nHTTP/1.1\nHost: data-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>RequestExpired</Code>\n  <Message>Request has expired</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 6: Invalid Date Format\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\nX-Amz-Date: July-14-2025\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidDateFormat</Code>\n  <Message>Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Implementation\n\nError responses are generated by two implementations:\n\n1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n   ```go\n   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\nBoth implementations ensure:\n- Consistent Content-Type header\n- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n- S3-compatible format\n\n## Testing\n\nTo run the error response test suite:\n\n```bash\n# Run all rejection tests\ngo test -v -run \"TestInvalidCredentialRejection|TestMalformedSignatureRejection\" ./internal/server/\n\n# Run headers consistency test\ngo test -v -run TestErrorResponseHeadersConsistency ./internal/server/\n\n# Run integration tests (requires INTEGRATION_TEST=1)\nINTEGRATION_TEST=1 go test -v -run TestInvalidCredentialsIntegration ./internal/server/\n```\n\n## Maintenance\n\nWhen adding new error scenarios:\n\n1. **Add test coverage** - Create tests in appropriate test file\n2. **Verify error code** - Use existing S3 error code when possible\n3. **Check performance** - Ensure response time < Authorization header missing\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Access key not found\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature verification failed\n- `InvalidAlgorithm` - Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm\n- `IncompleteSignature` - Authorization header missing fields\n- `RequestExpired` - Request timestamp outside ±15min window\n- `MissingDateHeader` - X-Amz-Date header missing\n- `InvalidDateFormat` - X-Amz-Date format invalid\n- `AccessDenied` - ACL-based access control rejection\n\n### Client Input Errors (400 Bad Request / 405 Method Not Allowed)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 400 Bad Request (or 405 Method Not Allowed)\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InvalidRequest` (400) - Unsupported POST operation, invalid parameters\n- `InvalidRange` (400) - Invalid Range header format\n- `MethodNotAllowed` (405) - HTTP method not supported\n\n### Resource Not Found Errors (404 Not Found)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 404 Not Found\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `NoSuchKey` - Object does not exist\n- `NoSuchBucket` - Bucket does not exist\n- `NoSuchUpload` - Multipart upload ID not found\n\n### Conditional Request Errors (412 Precondition Failed)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 412 Precondition Failed\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `PreconditionFailed` - If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed\n\n### Internal Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InternalError` - Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors\n\n### Service Unavailable (503 Service Unavailable)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `ServiceUnavailable` - Health check failures\n\n## Consistency Verification Results\n\n### ✅ Consistent Elements\n\n1. **Content-Type Header**\n   - All error responses set `Content-Type: application/xml`\n   - No exceptions or variations\n   - Verified across both `writeError` implementations\n\n2. **HTTP Status Codes**\n   - Appropriate for each error category\n   - Consistent within each category (all auth errors are 403, all not found are 404, etc.)\n\n3. **Response Format**\n   - Authorization header missing fields\n- `RequestExpired` - Request timestamp outside ±15min window\n- `MissingDateHeader` - X-Amz-Date header missing\n- `InvalidDateFormat` - X-Amz-Date format invalid\n- `AccessDenied` - Unsupported POST operation, invalid parameters\n- `InvalidRange` (400) - Invalid Range header format\n- `MethodNotAllowed` (405) - HTTP method not supported\n\n### Resource Not Found Errors (404 Not Found)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 404 Not Found\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `NoSuchKey` - Object does not exist\n- `NoSuchBucket` - Bucket does not exist\n- `NoSuchUpload` - Multipart upload ID not found\n\n### Conditional Request Errors (412 Precondition Failed)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 412 Precondition Failed\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `PreconditionFailed` - If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed\n\n### Internal Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InternalError` - Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors\n\n### Service Unavailable (503 Service Unavailable)\n\nAll these errors return **consistent headers:**\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `ServiceUnavailable` - Health check failures\n\n## Consistency Verification Results\n\n### ✅ Consistent Elements\n\n1. **Content-Type Header**\n   - All error responses set `Content-Type: application/xml`\n   - No exceptions or variations\n   - Verified across both `writeError` implementations\n\n2. **HTTP Status Codes**\n   - Appropriate for each error category\n   - Consistent within each category (all auth errors are 403, all not found are 404, etc.)\n\n3. **Response Format**\n   - PASS (8/8 scenarios)\n✅ All Content-Type headers consistent\n✅ All responses under 100ms threshold\n```\n\n## Implementation Verification\n\n### Server Handler (server.go:797-805)\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    var codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n    xml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n    xml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n        codeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n```\n\n### Handlers Package (handlers/handlers.go:2696-2704)\n```go\nfunc (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    var codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n    xml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n    xml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n        codeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n```\n\n**Result:** ✅ Both implementations are identical and produce consistent headers\n\n## Conclusion\n\nARMOR error response headers are **fully consistent** across all rejection types:\n\n1. ✅ All error responses set exactly the same headers (`Content-Type: application/xml` plus HTTP status)\n2. ✅ Header values are consistent and appropriate for each error category\n3. ✅ Response format is standardized S3 XML error structure\n4. ✅ No functional inconsistencies found\n5. ✅ Performance requirements met (all responses < 100ms)\n\nThe only minor issue is code duplication between two identical `writeError` functions, which is a maintainability concern but not a functional inconsistency.\n\n**Overall Assessment: PASS** -
